title: Rogue Trader
wikitext:
{{External|rs}}
{{Has quick guide}}
[[File:Ali's Discount Wares..png|300px|right|thumb|Ali's Discount Wares in Al Kharid.]]
[[File:Rogue Trader logo.jpg|left]]
'''Rogue Trader''' is a series of miniquest-like errands one can undertake after completing [[The Feud]] quest. It was released on [[15 August]] [[2005]]. There are 3 parts to Rogue Trader, each one unlocking additional stock from [[Ali Morrisane]]'s store.

'''Note:''' Before you can do any of the parts listed below you have to talk to Ali Morrisane in Al Kharid.

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = Speak to [[Ali Morrisane]] in [[Al Kharid]]
|difficulty = Easy
|requirements = Completion of following quests:
*[[The Feud]]
*[[Rune Mysteries]]
*[[Icthlarin's Little Helper]] - gives access to [[Sophanem]]
**[[Gertrude's Cat]]
|items = *3 [[dyes]] of any colour (to unlock [[Menaphite clothing]]) OR 3 animal pelts of the same kind (to unlock the [[Desert outfit]]). This includes [[cowhide]], [[grey wolf fur]], [[snakeskin]], [[bear fur]], or [[wool]] (not [[balls of wool]])
*100 coins (for blackjack street urchin, optional)
|recommended = '''Recommended'''
*~1,000 coins for carpet rides to [[Pollnivneach]] and Sophanem (and back to [[Shantay Pass]])
*[[Ring of dueling]] to quickly get to [[Al Kharid]]
*A teleport to [[Varrock]]
|length = Short (if fully prepared) - Medium
|leagueRegion = {{LeagueRegion|Desert}} (Cannot be fully completed, as Misthalin is required for the rune shop section)
}}

==Runes==
After completing [[Rune Mysteries]], talk to [[Ali Morrisane]]. He will tell you to talk to [[Aubury]] in [[Varrock]] about a rune deal. After doing so return to Ali. He will say that there is an odd lock mechanism on the boxes of runes. Say that you would like to see his selection of runes. You can pick between buying elemental runes or higher level runes, and then trying to open a small or large casket of runes.

The small casket is easier to open than the large one. For both, you get these three options:

The '''"Pick Lock"''' option has a higher chance of success with a higher [[Thieving]] level and has a chance to break the [[lockpick]] if one is used; the '''"Force Box"''' option will have a higher chance of success with a higher [[Strength]] level. It is impossible to open the large casket by choosing either of these options. However, it is possible to open a small one with those options.
[[File:Rogue Trader - Sudoku.png|thumb|The Sudoku screen.]]Once you click '''"Examine Lock"''' you should see a screen that looks like this, but with an incomplete sudoku puzzle.
[[File:Rogue Trader - Completed Sudoku.png|thumb|A completed Sudoku screen.]]

The runes substitute numbers in a standard sudoku puzzle. You can either solve the sudoku puzzle yourself or use a sudoku solver ("runedoku solver"). The main objective is to line up the runes so that no runes are repeated in any row, line, or 3x3 grid.

After solving the sudoku puzzle, you will have the options of either choosing to buy all the runes for a specified discounted price or to see what the runes are first. If you decide to see the runes first, you lose a small portion of your discount - the difference between buying all of the runes before seeing them turns out to be on average 6-10% cheaper than buying them all afterwards.''' '''However, the prices the runes will be at if you choose to see them will still be a lot cheaper than what Ali sells them for when you do not complete a sudoku.

===Strategy===
A trick that works with the smaller rune caskets is to position the runes as shown.

:1 2 3 4
:4 3 2 1
:3 4 1 2
:2 1 4 3

The placement of the starting runes varies, but the pattern is always the same. This pattern can always be performed, regardless of starting placement.

==Blackjacks==
Head south to the town of [[Pollnivneach]] and talk to the [[Blackjack seller]] who is outside, just south of [[Ali the Kebab seller]]. Go through the conversation with him, and choose either 'cunning plan' option for defensive blackjacks or ask him to knock you out for offensive blackjacks.

If choosing the cunning plan, talk to the urchin and pay him 100 coins to make him help you convince the Blackjack salesman that he needs to produce some different equipment. There will be a short cutscene. After that cutscene ends, talk to the Blackjack seller again.

If choosing to get knocked out, simply go through the dialogue.

Once you return to Al-Kharid, Ali Morrisane will still ask that you return to Pollnivneach and ask the Blackjack seller to expand his wares further. This is optional and will switch Ali Morrisane's inventory between offensive and defensive blackjacks.

==Clothes==
Talk to [[Siamun]] in the building east of the silk stall with the spinning wheel in [[Sophanem]]. If you have completed [[Icthlarin's Little Helper]], you can take a carpet from south [[Pollnivneach]] into [[Sophanem]] or you can use the [[Pharaoh's sceptre]] to teleport to [[Jalsavrah]] ([[Pyramid Plunder]]).

Siamun is a rude, gruff character who wants either three dyes or three hides '''of the same animal'''. Siamun will accept [[cow hide|cowhide]], [[grey wolf fur]], [[snakeskin]] (not un-tanned [[snake hide]]), [[bear fur]], or [[wool]] (not [[balls of wool]]). He will not accept bank notes. Giving him dyes will cause Morrisane to stock [[Menaphite clothing]] and giving the hides will stock [[Desert outfit]]. Both sets are cosmetic variants of [[Desert clothing]] with identical stats.

Once you have given Siamun the items, he will begin shipping clothing.

If you talk to [[Ali Morrisane]] back in Al-Kharid, he will ask that you return to [[Sophanem]] and ask [[Siamun]] to make additional clothing. This is optional and will switch Morrisane's inventory between the two outfits.

==Completion!==
After completing all three tasks (rune boxes, blackjacks, and clothes), you now can buy blackjacks, clothes, and runes (including cosmics and souls etc.) from Ali '''''without''''' having to do the runes puzzles again. 

==Post-errand task==
After helping [[Ali Morrisane]] with runes, blackjacks and clothing, and completing [[Prince Ali Rescue]], you may engage in optional, additional dialogue with any shop owner within [[Al Kharid]], asking about their opinion of Ali. They will all claim that he is a shifty man, and that he's brought four men from the south to set up business, and has sent them to all the shop owners around Al Kharid to threaten them if they do not work for Ali Morrisane.

These four men are [[Ali the Farmer]], [[Ali the Guard]], [[Ali the Smith]] and [[Ali the Tailor]], who appear south of Ali Morrisane's stall immediately after you complete his three errands. Speak to any of them, saying that you have heard that they work for Ali Morrisane. They will say that he has assisted them in setting up shop in exchange for a cut of their profits.

Speak to Ali Morrisane. Tell him that you have heard of his plans of expanding business, and that his men are threatening the other shopkeepers. He will respond by expressing his regret over the situation, explaining that his men are simply overenthusiastic. He then reveals that his ultimate goal is to take control of all the shops in Al Kharid to put an end to these issues.
